rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers when referring to component ports in AGREE contracts
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when modifying AADL models
Ensure all AGREE variables are grounded in the current model or explicitly declared
Avoid direct references to AADL properties within AGREE expressions unless exposed as ports or constants
Use valid AADL scopes for AGREE annex blocks
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting new AGREE bindings
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Avoid direct references to AADL properties within AGREE expressions
Do not ground AGREE variables in AADL properties without explicit port or constant exposure
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
